compiled_state.title: Prevent the explosion of documentation created by LLMs
compiled_state.description:
## Context

Dev agents emit low-quality documentation — padded prose, broken links, orphaned sections, and stale examples — that accumulates unreviewed across sprints. Code reviewers currently lack a dedicated lens for documentation quality, and test quality reviewers miss assertion-less test bodies. This epic adds three complementary prevention layers without introducing new commit-blocking gates: (1) a doc overlay review agent that flags documentation quality issues during code review at conservative severity, (2) two additions to the standard code reviewer targeting LLM-specific hygiene patterns, and (3) a T7 pattern in the test quality overlay for assertion-less test bodies.

## Success Criteria

**SC1.** A new doc overlay agent is added with YAML frontmatter `name: code-reviewer-doc` and `model: sonnet`. It fires when the review classifier emits `doc_overlay: true`. The agent evaluates staged `.md` files against the following patterns, all treated as probabilistic — the agent must consider document context before flagging and must not assume a pattern constitutes bloat without examining its surroundings:

- D2: broken internal links (links that resolve to an anchor or file path not present in the repository snapshot visible to the agent)
- D4: duplicate headings within a single file (two or more headings at the same level with identical text — may be intentional in reference docs or changelogs)
- D5: file exceeds a large-file threshold with no internal heading structure (may be intentional for flat reference files)
- P-D1: orphaned section — a heading whose body prose has no cross-references to other sections and no apparent standalone reader utility
- P-D2: padded prose — filler phrases that add length without adding information (e.g., "It is important to note that", "As mentioned above")
- P-D3: stale example — a code block referencing a symbol, CLI flag, or configuration key not present in the diff-visible codebase
- P-D4: concept drift — the same term used with materially different meanings in different sections of the same document

All findings from this agent are emitted at `important` or `minor` severity — never `critical`. The Anti-Manufacturing Directive applies: an empty findings array is a valid and expected output for most diffs.

**SC2.** The review classifier is updated to emit `doc_overlay: true` when the staged diff contains at least one `.md` file in scope. Scope is defined by exclusion: the classifier must NOT emit `doc_overlay: true` for diffs containing only (a) any directory containing a `SKILL.md` file and all subdirectories thereof, (b) any `.md` file with YAML frontmatter containing both `name:` and `model:` keys (agent definition files), or (c) any `.md` file located in a `workflows/` or `prompts/` subdirectory.

**SC3.** The standard code reviewer's delta file — identified by `name: code-reviewer-standard` in its YAML frontmatter, or by the filename pattern `code-reviewer-standard.delta.md` in the agent composition directory — is updated to add two hygiene checklist items: (a) paranoid null checks: null-guard branches on values whose types guarantee non-null at the call site; (b) vestigial error handlers: catch-log-rethrow blocks where the catch body adds no recovery logic beyond re-throwing. Both items emit `hygiene` category findings at `important` severity. The update is additive. The agent composition build process (the script that assembles delta files into compiled agent files) successfully regenerates the compiled agent file without errors.

**SC4.** The test quality overlay agent's delta file — identified by `name: code-reviewer-test-quality` in its YAML frontmatter, or by the filename pattern `code-reviewer-test-quality.delta.md` in the agent composition directory — is updated to add Pattern T7: assertion-less test body (a test function whose body contains no assertion statement, mock verification call, or exception expectation). T7 findings are emitted at `critical` severity in the `verification` category. The update is additive. The agent composition build process successfully regenerates the compiled agent file without errors.

**SC5.** A before/after validation run is conducted using a synthetic diff staged in a test repository. The diff contains: (a) a `.md` file in `docs/` with a broken internal link, a paragraph of padded prose, and a duplicate heading, (b) a Python test file with one assertion-less test function, and (c) a Python source file with a null check on a value typed as non-optional. The doc overlay agent correctly flags the broken link (D2) and padded prose (P-D2) as `important`-severity findings, and includes the duplicate heading (D4) as an `important` or `minor` finding noting it may be intentional. The test quality overlay correctly flags the assertion-less test (T7) at `critical` severity. The standard code reviewer correctly flags the null check as a paranoid null check finding at `important` severity.
